codeReview.summary: Pass. The later feature-scoped commit `8ba4387` supersedes the stale handoff commit by completing the split of the oversized `panel-core` extracts into focused modules, and the current versions of those affected modules remain within the hard <=200-line limit. In the reviewed/current code, `panel-core.ts` is a 36-line entrypoint that loads the refactor modules (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/panel-core.ts:24-30`), `event-handler.ts` delegates listener wiring to the extracted listener modules (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/event-handler.ts:23-28`), and `message-processor.ts` still routes the sidepanel runtime message types to the same behaviors through dedicated handlers (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/message-processor.ts:42-86`). The sidepanel bootstrap still imports `panel-modules` before constructing `SidePanelUI` (`packages/extension/sidepanel/panel.ts:1,16`), `state-manager.ts` still initializes lifecycle/listener wiring (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/state-manager.ts:20-38`), `event-handler-runtime.ts` still forwards Chrome runtime/storage events into the panel (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/event-handler-runtime.ts:16-29`), and `message-handler-final.ts` still renders assistant output, appends context, and records usage (`packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core/message-handler-final.ts:51-59`). I also compared current HEAD to `8ba4387`; the follow-up changes in the affected modules are formatting/import-order adjustments only, and the current affected module sizes still top out at 193 lines (`event-handler-settings.ts`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state is stale for this feature: the later corrective commit `8ba4387` appears to be the real feature-scoped completion point, but the mission still records the earlier incomplete handoff commit `28a625d`, which led scrutiny to review the wrong artifact.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` records `completedWorkerSessionId: "daa6c021-818d-464a-8a20-f1599ec1e576"`, that handoff JSON points to commit `28a625dc4f1bcc1172fa18867c445054d6d27589`, and the original scrutiny report reviewed that commit. `git log --oneline -- packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/core` shows `8ba4387 refactor(panel-core): split 1542-line panel-core.ts into 19 focused modules` immediately after `28a625d`. Searches of `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs` and `worker-transcripts.jsonl` found no mission record for the later session/commit, so the mission metadata never advanced to the superseding artifact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature completion tracking should cross-check handoff completeness fields before marking a feature complete; the recorded handoff explicitly said the 200-line target was still unmet and returned to the orchestrator, but the feature remained marked completed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The recorded handoff file `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T12-11-15-272Z__sidepanel-refactor-panel-core__daa6c021-818d-464a-8a20-f1599ec1e576.json` has `returnToOrchestrator: true`, `whatWasLeftUndone` saying `event-handler.ts` and `message-processor.ts` still exceeded 200 lines, and a non-blocking discovered issue about those oversized modules; despite that, `features.json` shows `status: "completed"` for `sidepanel-refactor-panel-core`.

summary: Supplemental pass: commit `8ba4387` appears to supersede the stale handoff commit used by the original review. In `8ba4387` and the current affected `panel-core` modules, the refactor achieves the intended focused-module split, keeps every affected file at or below 200 lines, and preserves the sidepanel bootstrap, event registration, runtime message routing, and assistant rendering flows.
